title: PROJECT_STATE
description: Portfolio-wide, project-agnostic schema for the machine-readable single source of truth each project maintains as PROJECT_STATE.json. Validated by project tooling before any agent begins implementation. Strip the _comment fields in production instances — they are schema-level documentation only.
_comment_bump_policy: schema_version follows semver. PATCH: add an optional field no tool branches on, tighten a pattern, fix a description. MINOR: add an optional field that introduces a new referenced layer (tooling reads it), add a required field with a default-safe value, rename enum member (deprecate old name first). MAJOR: remove a required field, change an enum in a breaking way, restructure a required object. Bump schema_version in PROJECT_STATE.json AND update $id here in the same commit. Never silently diverge. v1.1.0 (2026-06-29, substrate v2.2): added optional `ratified_specs` (top-level) + `promotes_to` (per packet) for the OpenSpec specs/-vs-changes graft — both optional, so v1.0.x instances still validate.

project.description: Stable project identity. Change slug only with a Decision Journal entry — it is used as the .keys/<slug> secrets folder name.

slug.description: kebab-case; used as the .keys/<slug> secrets folder name and in CI paths

one_liner.description: One sentence describing what the project does for its primary user

domain.description: Problem domain or industry vertical, kept generic (e.g. 'developer tooling', 'knowledge management')

schema_version.description: semver of this PROJECT_STATE document's shape. Bump per _comment_bump_policy above whenever the schema changes. Current canonical version: 1.1.0.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) this file was last hand-edited. Not auto-generated; the human or agent updating the file sets this.

current_stage.description: Lifecycle stage. substrate = research synthesized, no implementation yet. design = architecture decided, no code. prototype = code exists, not production-hardened. mvp = first shippable slice. beta = externally testable. ga = generally available. maintenance = feature-stable, defect-fix only.

strategic_posture.description: North-star positioning. Every feature packet should be traceable to the hard_promise.

hard_promise.description: The one thing users can always count on — the invariant the project never breaks

wedge.description: The narrow initial use-case that proves the hard promise before scaling

what_it_is_not.description: Explicit anti-scope statements. Prevents duplicate builds and scope creep.

source_of_truth_order.description: Ordered list of doc paths or symbolic names. When two sources conflict, the one earlier in this list wins. Agents must consult this before resolving ambiguities.

canonical_docs.description: Authoritative, current documents. Agents build only from these. Documents not listed here are treated as historical or speculative.

status.description: current = finalized and enforced. draft = in progress. review = awaiting sign-off.

must_read.description: If true, every agent MUST read this document before beginning any task in the project. Default false.

historical_docs.description: Superseded or evidence-only documents. Reference as evidence; never build directly from them. Include a note explaining why each is here rather than canonical.

note.description: Why this document is historical rather than canonical

active_feature_packets.description: All feature packets, including planned and done. The canonical list of what has been built or is being built.

id.description: Packet identifier: three-digit zero-padded number followed by a kebab-case slug, e.g. '001-context-substrate'. The numeric prefix determines precedence when two packets overlap.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the packet directory, e.g. 'docs/features/001-context-substrate'

status.description: planned = not started. active = in progress. review-ready = implementation done, awaiting review gates. blocked = cannot proceed (see blocked_on in packet's status.json). done = all AC met, all review gates passed. superseded = replaced by another packet.

owner_model_tier.description: Recommended model tier per the AI session-model workflow doctrine. E.g. 'opus-plan/sonnet-implement', 'sonnet', 'local'. Not enforced by schema; advisory.

blocked_on.description: Packet ids or named blockers preventing progress. Only relevant when status is 'blocked'.

promotes_to.description: Capability name(s) (matching ratified_specs[].capability) this packet's behavior-spec delta promotes into when it reaches 'done'. Absent/empty for packets that ratify no standing behavior (e.g. pure refactors). Substrate v2.2.

current_focus.description: The single active workstream. Agents should not start new packets unless current_focus is done or blocked.

packet_id.description: Must match an id in active_feature_packets

summary.description: One sentence describing what is being done right now

next_product_slice.description: The packet queued to start after current_focus is done. Provides a clear handoff for session resumption.

packet_id.description: Must match an id in active_feature_packets with status 'planned'

summary.description: One sentence describing what the next slice delivers

do_not_start.description: Explicit hold list. Things no agent should build yet. Each entry must name a reason — 'blocked on X', 'deferred to phase N', 'decision pending DJ-NNN'. An empty array is valid and means nothing is on hold.

reason.description: Why it is on hold — must be specific enough to know when the hold lifts

commands.description: Named paste-ready commands for common project operations (dev server, test, lint, build, migrate, etc.). Empty object while no runnable app exists. Agents use these rather than inferring commands from context.

examples.dev: run in your own CMD window — see AGENTS.md §LRP

gates.description: Checks that must pass before any feature packet is marked 'done'. Ordered: run in listed order. A gate with blocking:true halts promotion to 'done' on failure; blocking:false emits a warning.

name.description: Human-readable gate name, e.g. 'Tier 0 linters', 'Schema validation', 'Test suite'

command.description: Paste-ready command to run this gate from the project root

blocking.description: true = gate failure blocks 'done'; false = warning only (advisory)

agent_rules.description: Hard rules every coding agent must obey when working in this project. Vendor-neutral. Supplement (not replace) the portfolio-wide rules in ~/shared/AGENTS.md. Each rule is a self-contained imperative statement.
examples: Never modify files outside allowed_edit_paths for the active feature packet.
Read PROJECT_STATE.json and the active packet's requirements.md before writing any code.
Run all gates before marking a packet done.
Secrets belong in .keys/<project-slug>/.env.local — never inside the repo tree.
Production state paths (state/, _meta/, registry/) require tmp_path isolation during dev and test.

ratified_specs.description: OpenSpec-style ratified current-behavior specs (substrate v2.2 graft), one entry per capability. specs/<capability>/spec.md holds the system's CURRENT behavior in Requirement/Scenario form, accumulated as feature packets reach 'done' and their deltas are promoted in. Distinct from canonical_docs (prose intent) — this is the behavior-spec layer and the authority for 'what the system does now'. OPTIONAL until the project has >=1 packet at status 'done'; the drift gate warns once shipped behavior exists without a ratified spec.

capability.description: kebab-case capability name; matches the specs/<capability>/ directory

path.description: Repo-relative path to the capability spec, e.g. 'specs/auth/spec.md'

status.description: ratified = current enforced behavior. draft = being ratified (a packet delta not yet promoted). superseded = capability retired.

promoted_from.description: Packet ids whose Requirement/Scenario deltas have been promoted into this spec.
